mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which weakens consumer spending and predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or unemployment rate declines without corresponding equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production (supply) and housing starts (demand) signals robust economic activity, which drives corporate earnings and predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.05 for any 3-year window, or 20d returns in co-moving periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising inflation (CPI) and falling industrial production (supply) indicates stagflation, which erodes corporate margins and predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or inflation-supply divergences fail to correlate with equity returns (Pearson r < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in residential construction (HOUST) reflects strong demand for housing, which boosts related industries and predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 for 1-year rolling window, or 5d returns in high HOUST periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) below 100,000 new jobs per month signals weak labor market performance, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or payroll declines fail to correlate with equity returns (Pearson r < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong manufacturing activity, which drives corporate profits and predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 for 1-year rolling window, or 20d returns in high INDPRO periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising consumer spending (RSAFS) and declining GDP growth (demand) indicates overleveraged households, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or consumption-demand divergences fail to correlate with equity returns (Pearson r < 0.1).
